questions.answer: Zeekr is a premium battery electric vehicle (BEV) brand under ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, which was incorporated in March 2021. It focuses on delivering high-performance, intelligent electric vehicles with advanced technology and innovative features. Zeekr’s product lineup includes models like the Zeekr 001, a premium crossover shooting brake, and the high-performance Zeekr 001 FR, both developed on the Sustainable Experience Architecture (SEA) platform. The brand emphasizes driving experience, charging solutions, and customer engagement, targeting global markets with a focus on innovation and sustainability. The information is based on knowledge available before 2024-12-31.

rag_info.chunk_content: Zeekr 001, Zeekr's first mass-produced battery electric vehicle (BEV), is a premium crossover shooting brake developed with proprietary research and development (R&D), deep market insights, and the Sustainable Experience Architecture (SEA) platform. Launched in April 2021 with deliveries beginning in October 2021, the Zeekr 001 was the first mass-produced BEV with over $1,000 kilometers CLTC range. In October 2023, Zeekr introduced the Zeekr 001 FR, a high-performance variant featuring a lightweight carbon fiber body, 800V Qilin battery, and quad e-drive system, achieving 2.02 seconds 0-100 kilometers per hour acceleration and a 280 kilometers per hour top speed. In 2024, Zeekr launched upgraded versions of the Zeekr 001 with Zeekr Intelligent Driving 2.0 and significant performance, safety, and intelligence upgrades.

Zeekr Technology Europe AB, or Zeekr Tech EU/ZTE, is Zeekr Technology's Sweden-based R&D center founded in 2013 that is strategically focused on the following aspects. Zeekr Technology Europe AB conducts R&D activities relating to the products that Zeekr Technology plans to roll out in overseas markets. These products include, among others, (i) next-generation mobility solutions, such as robotaxi, (ii) passenger battery electric vehicles (BEVs) satisfying local standards and customer preferences, and (iii) a series of digital offerings, such as data analytics tools and smart cockpit functions. ● Zeekr Technology Europe AB also implements the development of a variety of BEV-related technologies and capabilities, such as user-centric systems with in-vehicle, cloud, and mobile functionalities, in-vehicle centralized architecture, as well as a cloud-based data management platform for user experiences and data analytics. Zeekr Technology Europe AB also provides specific R&D services on a project basis. ● In addition, Zeekr Technology Europe AB has developed SEA-M, an advanced version of SEA featuring integration potential for autonomous driving systems and future smart mobility, to support Zeekr Technology’s next-generation BEVs. SEA-M is designed to enable mobility solutions that fulfill different use cases with superior driving experience, ultralong durability, and the capabilities for ongoing upgrades. BEVs developed on this platform, such as the Zeekr Mix and Waymo RT robotaxi, can have a number of innovative features, such as wide door openings with no B-pillar, highly flexible interiors, and advanced interior climate technologies. SEA-M will further facilitate the design for future BEV models.

ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited (the “Company” or “Zeekr”) was incorporated under the laws of the Cayman Islands on March 31, 2021. The Company, together with its subsidiaries (collectively, the “Group”), is principally engaged in the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles and batteries, as well as the provision of automotive-related research and development services. Prior to the establishment of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, the Group’s businesses were conducted through the following entities: (i) ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., previously known as Shanghai Maple Guorun Automobile Company Ltd. (“Zeekr Shanghai”); (ii) ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd. (“Zeekr Hangzhou Bay”); (iii) Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd. (“Ningbo Viridi”); (iv) ZEEKR Technology Europe AB (“ZTE”, previously known as China-Euro Vehicle Technology Aktiebolag or “CEVT”). In 2021, in an effort to establish an independent Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) business to obtain investment from outside investors and in preparation for an overseas listing, Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd. (“Geely Holding”), the controlling shareholder of Geely Automobile Holdings Limited (“Geely Auto”), undertook a series of contemplated reorganizations (the “Reorganization”). The main purpose of the Reorganization was to establish ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited as the overseas holding company for the above BEV-related businesses that were originally operated within Geely Holding and Geely Auto. Subsequent to the establishment of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited in March 2021, the above businesses were transferred to ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited by Geely Holding and Geely Auto as part of the Reorganization.

Lotus Technology is a fast-growing battery electric vehicle (BEV) technology company developing and offering next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions to lead the electrification, intelligentization, and innovation of the automobile industry. Lotus Technology is an independently-run startup-style company relying on its in-house research and development (R&D) capabilities and self-owned sales and marketing network, among others. Lotus Technology adopts a flat and efficient organizational structure led by key management with diversified backgrounds. Since inception, Lotus Technology has been managed and directed by its executive officers, and save for Conghui An, who is currently an executive director of Geely Auto, none of Lotus Technology's executive officers are members of management of Geely Auto. Additionally, Mr. An is expected to not hold any positions in Geely Auto prior to or upon the completion of the offering. While Lotus Technology's chairman, Shufu Li, is also the chairman of Geely Auto, upon the completion of the offering, the directors that Lotus Technology shares in common with Geely Auto will not have executive roles at Lotus Technology. Lotus Technology has been dedicated to serving its customers by leveraging top-notch technology, advanced product concepts, and an enriched entrepreneurial spirit that embraces creativity and innovation. ZEEKR is strategically positioned as a premium battery electric vehicle (BEV) brand that delivers an ultimate experience covering driving, charging, after-sale service, and customer community engagement. ZEEKR’s product family meets a wide spectrum of customer needs in different mobility and travel scenarios and is highly customized with a wide selection of vehicle configurations.

Lotus Technology began as a business unit within Geely Auto in October 2017. Lotus Technology conducts its business primarily through the following entities: (i) ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. (“ZEEKR Shanghai”), (ii) ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd. (“ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay”), (iii) Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd. (“Ningbo Viridi”) and (iv) Zeekr Technology Europe AB (“CEVT”). Under the leadership of the co-founders, Mr. Shufu Li, Mr. Conghui An, Mr. Donghui Li, and Mr. Shengyue Gui, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology was incorporated as an exempted company with limited liability in March 2021 under the law of the Cayman Islands to act as the holding company. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology is seeking to list separately from Geely Auto because of its different brand positioning and its operational, management, and financial independence. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology has an equity story built around its premium brand, product portfolio, and future plans that is better served by operating independently and seeking a separate listing, which the company believes will allow it to establish its own profile and attract different investors. In April 2021, ZEEKR Innovation, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology, was incorporated under the laws of the British Virgin Islands. In the same period, ZEEKR Technology, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Innovation, was incorporated under the laws of Hong Kong. In April 2021, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology announced the launch of its first BEV model, ZEEKR 001, and started delivery from October 2021.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Zeekr 001 
summary: The Zeekr 001, launched in April 2021 as the first mass-produced battery electric vehicle (BEV) on the SEA platform, is a premium crossover shooting brake known for its remarkable range of over 1,000 km on the CLTC cycle. In October 2023, the high-performance Zeekr 001 FR variant was unveiled, featuring a lightweight carbon fiber body, an 800V Qilin battery, and a quad e-drive system that enables acceleration from 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.02 seconds, with a top speed of 280 km/h. all_retrieved_content.page_content: Zeekr’s smart cockpit is powered by Zeekr OS, an exclusive distributed operating system designed for battery electric vehicles (BEVs), seamlessly integrated with Zeekr's state-of-the-art electrical and electronic (E/E) architecture. By dynamically allocating processing power to services, functions, and applications in real-time, Zeekr OS optimizes cockpit interactions, autonomous driving, and vehicle control, ensuring a seamless and highly responsive user experience. Zeekr OS features a unified service-oriented architecture, in-house full-stack research and development (R&D), optimized network diagnostics, and automated software integration testing, eliminating system lag and enabling continuous updates. With multi-kernel compatibility (Linux, QNX), Zeekr OS supports 360-degree surround-view monitoring, parking assistance, and intelligent energy management, setting a new benchmark for BEV software architecture. The smart cockpit experience is further enhanced by the Qualcomm 8295 SoC-based platform, standard across all Zeekr vehicle models launched in 2024, with the Zeekr 009 Grand featuring the world’s first dual-8295 configuration. The Zeekr Sound audio system, equipped with AI-powered tuning, delivers premium acoustic performance, while the Human-Machine Interface (HMI) 3.5 introduces 3D navigation, advanced visual effects, and an intuitive Eva family interface. EVA, the AI-powered driving assistant, leverages machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and computer vision to provide intelligent voice interaction, predictive assistance, and customizable AI personas. Additionally, Zeekr’s open cockpit ecosystem integrates a virtualized mobile platform, a Scenario Library, and Internet of Things (IoT) connectivity, allowing seamless integration with smart home devices, gaming, and entertainment applications such as Kid Zone, Tencent Cloud Gaming, and wireless karaoke.

title: Smart Cockpit 
summary: Zeekr's smart cockpit, set to launch in 2024, is powered by Zeekr OS, a specialized distributed operating system designed for battery electric vehicles (BEVs). This system integrates advanced electronic and electrical architecture, enabling real-time processing power allocation to enhance user interactions, autonomous driving, and vehicle control. Key features include a unified service-oriented architecture, in-house R&D, improved network diagnostics, and automated software integration testing, which together minimize system lag and support continuous updates. The multi-kernel compatibility allows for advanced functionalities like 360-degree surround-view monitoring and intelligent energy management, setting a new benchmark for BEV software.

The smart cockpit experience is further elevated by the Qualcomm 8295 SoC platform, particularly in the Zeekr 009 Grand, which boasts a dual-8295 configuration. The Zeekr Sound audio system delivers premium sound through AI-driven tuning, while the HMI 3.5 offers advanced 3D navigation and visual effects, enhanced by the intuitive Eva family interface. EVA, the AI assistant, leverages machine learning and natural language processing for intelligent voice interactions and customizable AI personas. The open cockpit ecosystem promotes a virtualized mobile platform and IoT connectivity, allowing integration with smart home devices and entertainment options like Kid Zone, Tencent Cloud Gaming, and wireless karaoke. Overall, Zeekr's smart cockpit, driven by Zeekr OS and AI advancements, aims to provide an immersive and connected driving experience.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Zeekr Tech EU 
summary: Zeekr Technology Europe AB (Zeekr Tech EU), established in 2013 in Sweden, serves as a research and development hub focused on creating innovative products for international markets. The organization specializes in next-generation mobility solutions, including robotaxis and battery electric vehicles (BEVs) tailored to local standards, alongside digital offerings like data analytics and smart cockpit functions. A significant focus is placed on advancing BEV technologies that prioritize user-centric systems, integrating in-vehicle, cloud, and mobile functionalities with centralized architecture for improved user experiences.

Central to Zeekr Tech EU's initiatives is the SEA-M platform, an evolution of the SEA architecture designed to support autonomous driving and smart mobility. This platform aims to deliver versatile mobility options, exceptional driving experiences, and long-lasting vehicles that can be continuously upgraded. BEVs developed on this platform, such as the Zeekr Mix and Waymo RT robotaxi, feature innovative designs, including wide door openings and flexible interiors. By the end of 2024, the company employed 733 individuals, focusing on mobility technologies, digital solutions, and future product development.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Customers try out Zeekr products and technologies, as well as participate in a wide range of events in the Zeekr Space, which usually occupies 100 square meters to 300 square meters in commercial areas. Lotus Technology has also launched Pop-Up Zeekr Spaces in China, which have more flexible leasing terms than the usual Zeekr Space and help the company connect with an expanded customer base. Zeekr Delivery Center. Lotus Technology utilizes Zeekr Delivery Centers for product delivery, most of which are located at facilities with large areas outside urban commercial centers to allow for vehicle storage and simultaneous delivery. Zeekr House. Lotus Technology launches additional Zeekr Houses in areas where car dealer shops are located across different tiers of cities in China, which allow the company to utilize the cluster effect and provide customers with a superior one-stop experience. Leveraging the service network of its sister brands in Geely Group, Lotus Technology plans to develop its Zeekr Houses cost-effectively and rapidly with the Zeekr brand and design. Through the Zeekr APP, prospective customers can place orders by paying a nonrefundable order deposit or production deposit. Relying on Lotus Technology's strong supply chain management capabilities inherited from Geely Group, Lotus Technology enables customers to choose from a vast pool of configurations, such as vehicle color, wheel hub size and style, air suspension system option, automatic door option, sound system, intelligent air conditioning package, and seat ventilation.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Direct Sales and Service Model 
summary: The company adopts a customer-centric go-to-market strategy, spearheaded by a skilled in-house sales and marketing team of 7,134 members, with a significant majority having extensive experience in the automobile and retail sectors. This team oversees a direct-to-consumer (DTC) sales network, which includes 538 offline locations—467 in China and 71 internationally—designed to provide a comprehensive, high-quality customer experience throughout the sales lifecycle. Key components of this network include the Zeekr Center, which serves as a premium showroom and community hub, and Zeekr Space, where customers can engage with products and attend events. Additionally, Zeekr Delivery Centers facilitate efficient product delivery, while Zeekr House locations are strategically positioned near car dealerships to enhance customer service through a one-stop experience. 

The Zeekr APP further streamlines the purchasing process, allowing customers to place orders with a nonrefundable deposit and customize their vehicle configurations. Following a 72-hour confirmation period, delivery specialists assist with pre-delivery arrangements and coordinate the final delivery, ensuring a seamless transition from order to ownership. This integrated approach not only enhances customer engagement but also leverages the Geely Group's supply chain to deliver value-added services efficiently.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Within less than three years since ZEEKR’s inception, ZEEKR has launched multiple commercialized electric vehicle models, namely ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan model. ZEEKR 001 is a five-seater crossover shooting brake BEV model targeting the premium market and mainly addressing the customer need for practical yet stylish traveling. ZEEKR released ZEEKR 001 (2024 model) in February 2024 and started vehicle delivery in March 2024. ZEEKR 009 is a luxury six-seater MPV addressing the customer need for luxury mobility. In April 2023, ZEEKR released ZEEKR X, the compact SUV model, and began to deliver ZEEKR X in June 2023. ZEEKR also started to deliver ZEEKR 001 FR in November 2023 and began to deliver the first upscale sedan model in January 2024. In addition to major upgrades such as the release of ZEEKR 001 (2024 model), ZEEKR has also been continuously fine-tuning and improving the performance of other BEV models. For example, ZEEKR updated the interior configurations of ZEEKR 009 and improved the chassis system of ZEEKR X in 2024. In April 2024, ZEEKR launched ZEEKR 009 Grand, a luxury version of ZEEKR 009 featuring enhanced safety, privacy, and intelligence. ZEEKR also released ZEEKR MIX, the MPV model, in the same month.

title: An Innovative Automotive Technology Company with Strong Product Offering to Capture Massive Premium BEV Market Opportunity 
summary: ZEEKR is an emerging battery electric vehicle (BEV) technology company dedicated to creating premium electric vehicles and innovative solutions that promote the electrification and intelligentization of the automotive sector. Operating as an independent startup, ZEEKR utilizes its in-house research and development capabilities alongside a proprietary sales and marketing network, all managed by a diverse and efficient leadership team. Notably, while some executives have ties to Geely Auto, they will not hold executive roles post-offering, ensuring a distinct operational identity.

Since its launch less than three years ago, ZEEKR has introduced several models, including the ZEEKR 001, a five-seater crossover, the luxury ZEEKR 009 MPV, and the compact ZEEKR X SUV, among others. The brand has garnered significant market attention, achieving rapid sales milestones, such as 10,000 deliveries of the ZEEKR 001 shortly after its release, positioning it as one of China’s fastest-selling premium BEVs.

ZEEKR vehicles are built on Geely Holding's proprietary SEA platform, which facilitates swift development and launch of new models while ensuring exceptional performance and user experience. The ZEEKR 001 and ZEEKR 009 are recognized for their impressive range capabilities, with the ZEEKR 009 being the first pure-electric MPV to exceed 800 km in range. Additionally, ZEEKR incorporates advanced autonomous driving technologies powered by cutting-edge hardware, enhancing both performance and safety.

The company emphasizes customer satisfaction through a focus on innovative technology and premium features, such as customizable in-vehicle configurations and luxurious amenities in models like the ZEEKR 009 Grand, which includes heated massaging seats and high-quality entertainment systems. Overall, ZEEKR is committed to delivering a holistic smart mobility experience, setting a high standard in the premium BEV market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: 1. Principal activities and history of the Group 
summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited, established on March 31, 2021, in the Cayman Islands, specializes in the commercialization and sales of electric vehicles and batteries, along with providing automotive-related research and development services through its subsidiaries. The company emerged from a reorganization initiated by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group Co., Ltd., which aimed to create an independent Battery Electric Vehicles (BEV) business to attract external investment and prepare for an overseas listing. This reorganization involved transferring various entities, including ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd., ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd., Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd., and ZEEKR Technology Europe AB, under the common control of Geely Holding to Zeekr.

In July 2021, ZEEKR established a wholly-owned subsidiary in China and subsequently acquired full equity stakes in Zeekr Hangzhou Bay and Zeekr Shanghai, incurring minor losses in additional paid-in capital. Additionally, Zeekr Shanghai acquired a 51% interest in Ningbo Viridi and later, in February 2022, ZTE from Geely Holding, further solidifying its operational structure. By May 2024, ZEEKR achieved a significant milestone by successfully listing on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), marking its growth and establishment in the electric vehicle market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: ZEEKR OS is the proprietary distributed battery electric vehicle (BEV) operating system running on Lotus Technology's E/E Architecture. ZEEKR OS improves Lotus Technology's BEVs’ processing capabilities across different scenarios by assigning appropriate capabilities to services, functions, or applications based on their real-time demands. For instance, ZEEKR OS optimizes the data transmission and processing for Lotus Technology's BEVs’ view cameras so that the data from these cameras can support the 360-degree display function and parking assistance function based on the actual system demand. ZEEKR OS runs on a number of mainstream kernels, such as Linux and QNX. Using core capabilities such as network diagnosis and management, service enhancement, safe management, and power management, ZEEKR OS achieves seamless connection among the kernel, services, and vehicle-level applications, such as cockpit services, autonomous driving, and energy services, thus ensuring safe, reliable, and accurate vehicle operation. In December 2023, Lotus Technology launched ZEEKR OS 5.1, an updated operating system with a variety of key updates, such as intelligent driving assistance and in-vehicle entertainment. metadata.title_summary:
title: ZEEKR OS 
summary: ZEEKR OS is a specialized operating system tailored for battery electric vehicles (BEVs), built on a distributed electronic and electrical (E/E) architecture. It optimizes processing capabilities by dynamically allocating resources to various services and applications based on real-time needs, such as enhancing data transmission for view cameras to support features like 360-degree displays and parking assistance. Compatible with popular kernels like Linux and QNX, ZEEKR OS ensures smooth connectivity among the kernel, services, and vehicle applications, which include cockpit services, autonomous driving, and energy management. Key functionalities of ZEEKR OS encompass network diagnostics, service enhancement, safe management, and power management, all aimed at maintaining safe and reliable vehicle operations. The recent launch of ZEEKR OS 5.1 in December 2023 brought significant updates, particularly in intelligent driving assistance and in-vehicle entertainment. The system's architecture is implemented using the ZEEA 3.0 framework, as illustrated in accompanying diagrams.

title: Visionary and Experienced Senior Leadership and World Class Management Team 
summary: Shufu Li, the founder and chairman of Zhejiang Geely Holding, has been instrumental in the company's establishment in 1986 and its impressive growth, which has earned it a spot on the Fortune 500 list for nine consecutive years. Conghui An, co-founder and CEO, brings over 25 years of experience within Geely Group, significantly contributing to the launch of brands like Lynk&Co. Together, they lead a management team that combines internal talent with seasoned external experts, fostering a rich blend of knowledge in automotive, consumer electronics, and software. This diverse expertise enhances Geely's competitive edge and accelerates its business acumen. Additionally, the CEVT team in Sweden, comprised of a motivated multinational group, leverages its proximity to Europe’s major car market to provide exceptional design and engineering capabilities for ZEEKR, driving innovation and future development in the automotive sector.

metadata.title_summary:
title: NOTES TO THE COMBINED AND CONSOLIDATED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS FOR THE YEARS ENDED DECEMBER 31, 2021, 2022 AND 2023 (Amounts in thousands, except share and per share data and otherwise noted) 
summary: The summary provides an overview of Geely Auto's performance-based Restricted Share Units (RSUs) and ZEEKR's financial metrics over the years 2021 to 2023. Geely Auto's RSU program, initiated in 2021, awarded 9,128,000 RSUs to management, employees, and directors, with vesting contingent on operational performance metrics such as sales volume and net profit. The RSUs vest over four years, starting from August 2021, with no reported forfeitures during this period. The program saw additional grants and transfers of RSUs in 2022 and 2023, resulting in a total of 4,854,000 unvested RSUs by the end of 2023, while maintaining a consistent fair value of RMB 22.3 per share.

In terms of financial performance, ZEEKR, established in March 2021, reported a significant increase in net losses from consolidated entities, rising from (2,299,923) thousand in 2021 to (8,264,191) thousand in 2023. The net loss attributable to ordinary shareholders also widened considerably during this period. The basic and diluted net loss per share increased each year, reaching (4.17) in 2023, with a stable weighted average of 2 billion shares outstanding for 2022 and 2023.

Additionally, the summary outlines the types of shares associated with RSUs, including shares issuable upon exercise, Series Pre-A preferred shares, and Series A preferred shares, each with distinct rights. ZEEKR's share figures showed growth from 52,964,800 in 2021 to 93,011,731 in 2023, reflecting operational progress. Overall, the data highlights Geely Auto's strategic employee compensation approach and ZEEKR's financial challenges, providing insights into their respective performance and growth trajectories.

Operating as an independent startup, ZEEKR utilizes its in-house research and development capabilities alongside a proprietary sales and marketing network, all managed by a diverse and efficient leadership team. Notably, while some executives have ties to Geely Auto, they will not hold executive roles post-offering, ensuring a distinct operational identity.

Since its launch less than three years ago, ZEEKR has introduced several models, including the ZEEKR 001, a five-seater crossover, the luxury ZEEKR 009 MPV, and the compact ZEEKR X SUV, among others. The brand has garnered significant market attention, achieving rapid sales milestones, such as 10,000 deliveries of the ZEEKR 001 shortly after its release, positioning it as one of China’s fastest-selling premium BEVs.

ZEEKR vehicles are built on Geely Holding's proprietary SEA platform, which facilitates swift development and launch of new models while ensuring exceptional performance and user experience. The ZEEKR 001 and ZEEKR 009 are recognized for their impressive range capabilities, with the ZEEKR 009 being the first pure-electric MPV to exceed 800 km in range. Additionally, ZEEKR incorporates advanced autonomous driving technologies powered by cutting-edge hardware, enhancing both performance and safety.

The company emphasizes customer satisfaction through a focus on innovative technology and premium features, such as customizable in-vehicle configurations and luxurious amenities in models like the ZEEKR 009 Grand, which includes heated massaging seats and high-quality entertainment systems. Overall, ZEEKR is committed to delivering a holistic smart mobility experience, setting a high standard in the premium BEV market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: ZEEKR's products have been well received by the market, achieving a total delivery of 10,000 units of ZEEKR 001 in less than four months since its initial delivery in October 2021, which, according to Frost & Sullivan, is one of the fastest among the major mid- to high-end new energy vehicle (NEV) models and premium BEV models in China. Lotus Technology's current and future models will be primarily based on Geely Holding’s proprietary SEA, which is highly agile, compatible, and enables Lotus Technology to quickly build and launch a wide range of vehicle models catering to different demands in the premium BEV segment. Developed based on SEA, ZEEKR vehicles embody impressive vehicle performance, FOTA-enabled upgrades, superior driving and riding experiences, as well as striking and trendy designs that provide a comprehensive smart mobility experience to Lotus Technology's customers. • Outstanding battery and range performance. The up-to- $100 \mathrm{kWh}$ battery supports a maximum CLTC range of $741 \mathrm{km}$ on ZEEKR 001 and $750 \mathrm{km}$ on ZEEKR 001 (2024 model), respectively, which is ahead of most of the BEV models of Lotus Technology's peers, according to Frost & Sullivan. According to Frost & Sullivan, ZEEKR 009 is the world’s first pure-electric MPV model with over $800 \mathrm{km}$ CLTC range, and it has the longest all-electric range in the MPV market by the end of February 2024. • State-of-the-art autonomous driving expertise.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology is a fast-growing BEV technology company. Through developing and offering next-generation premium BEVs and technology-driven solutions, Lotus Technology aspires to lead the electrification, intelligentization, and innovation of the automobile industry. Since its inception, Lotus Technology has focused on innovation in BEV architecture, hardware, software, and application of new technologies. The company's efforts are backed by strong in-house R&D capabilities, a deep understanding of products, high operational flexibility, and a flat, efficient organizational structure. Together, these features enable fast product development, launch, and iteration, as well as a series of customer-oriented products and go-to-market strategies. Thus, Lotus Technology is able to rapidly expand even with a limited operating history. Lotus Technology strategically spearheaded the premium intelligent BEV market with unique positioning, featuring a strong sense of technology, in-house R&D capabilities, stylish design, high-caliber performance, and a premium user experience. The current product portfolio primarily includes ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 001 FR, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan model. ZEEKR 001. With an unwavering commitment to Lotus Technology's mission, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 in April 2021, a five-seater, cross-over hatchback vehicle model with superior performance and functionality. Targeting the premium BEV market, ZEEKR 001 is Lotus Technology's first vehicle model and the world’s first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, according to Frost & Sullivan. ZEEKR 001 is also the first mass-produced BEV model with over $1,000 km CLTC range, according to Frost & Sullivan. Lotus Technology began the delivery of ZEEKR 001 in October 2021.

metadata.title_summary:
title: OVERVIEW 
summary: ZEEKR is an innovative and rapidly expanding battery electric vehicle (BEV) technology company committed to transforming the automobile industry through electrification and advanced technology. With a focus on next-generation premium BEVs, ZEEKR leverages strong in-house research and development capabilities, a flexible organizational structure, and a deep understanding of customer needs to efficiently develop and launch products. The company has established a prominent position in the premium BEV market with models such as the ZEEKR 001, ZEEKR 009, ZEEKR X, and an upscale sedan, all emphasizing stylish design, high performance, and exceptional user experiences.

Since its inception in October 2021, ZEEKR has achieved remarkable milestones, including the rapid delivery of 196,633 vehicles by December 2023, with 192,441 units sold in China alone. The ZEEKR 001, recognized as the world's first mass-produced pure electric shooting brake, has been particularly successful, reaching 10,000 units delivered within four months of its launch. The company benefits from the extensive resources and industry experience of its parent company, Geely Group, which enhances ZEEKR's product innovation and operational efficiency.

ZEEKR's technological advancements are supported by its proprietary SEA platform, enabling a diverse range of vehicle types and facilitating the integration of cutting-edge technologies. The company is also focused on enhancing the customer experience through a direct-to-consumer sales model and a comprehensive network of customer touchpoints across China. Additionally, ZEEKR is expanding its charging infrastructure and forming strategic partnerships with leading firms to support its growth and global expansion.

The BEV market is experiencing significant growth, particularly in China, where sales are projected to increase dramatically in the coming years. ZEEKR aims to capitalize on this trend by expanding its vehicle portfolio and exploring new markets, including the robotaxi sector in the United States. With a strong management team and a commitment to customer-centric operations, ZEEKR is well-positioned to lead the future of premium electric mobility.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In February 2024, Lotus Technology released an upgraded model of ZEEKR 001, or ZEEKR 001 (2024 model). Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 001 (2024 model) in March 2024. In October 2023, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR 001 FR, a cross-over hatchback vehicle model based on ZEEKR 001. Featuring unique exterior and interior design and proprietary technologies, ZEEKR 001 FR is designed to offer outstanding vehicle performance with various driving modes. Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 001 FR in November 2023. • ZEEKR 009. In November 2022, Lotus Technology launched its second model, ZEEKR 009, a luxury six-seater MPV model providing a comfortable, ultra-luxury mobility experience for both families and business uses. ZEEKR 009 is the world’s first premium MPV based on a pure-electric platform, according to Frost & Sullivan. ZEEKR 009 has enjoyed wide popularity since launch, and Lotus Technology started to deliver ZEEKR 009 to customers in January 2023. • ZEEKR X. In April 2023, Lotus Technology released ZEEKR X, a compact SUV model featuring spacious interior design, advanced technology, and superior driving performance. Lotus Technology began to deliver ZEEKR X in June 2023. • ZEEKR Upscale Sedan Model. In November 2023, Lotus Technology launched its first upscale sedan model targeting tech-savvy adults and families. Powered by 800V architecture and multi-link suspension structure, the upscale sedan model is expected to achieve a 2.84 s 0-100 km/h acceleration and a 688 km maximum CLTC range. Lotus Technology began the delivery of the first upscale sedan model in January 2024.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The development of Lotus Technology's BEV models is powered by SEA, a set of open-source, electric and modularized platforms owned by Geely Holding compatible with A segment to E segment, covering sedan, SUV, MPV, hatchback, roadster, pick-up truck, and robotaxi, which have a wheelbase mainly between 1,800 mm to 3,300 mm. Lotus Technology depends on Geely Holding to allow the company to continue to utilize SEA, which is currently the most suitable platform for Lotus Technology. SEA also offers the flexibility to quickly adopt and accommodate the latest and most advanced technology improvements. For example, Lotus Technology was able to equip ZEEKR 009 with CATL’s latest Qilin battery, making ZEEKR 009 the first mass-produced BEV model equipped with Qilin battery, according to Frost & Sullivan. Together with Lotus Technology's proprietary advanced battery solutions and highly efficient electric drive system, ZEEKR 009’s extended range version is the world’s first pure-electric MPV model with an over 800 km CLTC range and the longest all-electric range in the MPV market by the end of February 2024, according to Frost & Sullivan.

In addition to its vehicle offerings, ZEEKR emphasizes a customer-centric approach, utilizing a direct-to-consumer sales model and a robust charging network to enhance the user experience. The company has formed strategic partnerships with leading firms like Mobileye and Waymo to advance autonomous driving technologies and expand its global reach.

The BEV market is poised for significant growth, particularly in China and Europe, with projections indicating a substantial increase in sales by 2028. ZEEKR is well-positioned to capitalize on these opportunities, focusing on next-generation mobility solutions that combine luxury, performance, and advanced technology.

metadata.title_summary:
title: OUR VEHICLES 
summary: ZEEKR is strategically positioned in the premium battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, focusing on the design, engineering, and sale of advanced electric vehicles that enhance user experience and drivability. Leveraging its strong research and development capabilities and synergies with Geely Group, ZEEKR aims to tap into China's burgeoning premium BEV market, which is projected to grow significantly from 666,400 units in 2024 to 2,607,600 units by 2028, according to Frost & Sullivan.

Since its inception, ZEEKR has launched several models, starting with the ZEEKR 001 in 2021, followed by the upgraded 2024 model, the ZEEKR 009, and the compact SUV ZEEKR X. The company has also expanded its offerings with the ZEEKR 001 FR and an upscale sedan, reflecting its commitment to catering to diverse customer needs. As of December 31, 2023, ZEEKR has delivered a total of 196,633 vehicles, primarily in China, marking one of the fastest growth rates in the premium BEV segment.

The monthly delivery data from June 2023 to March 2024 shows a consistent upward trend, with March 2024 witnessing 13,012 units delivered. This growth is indicative of rising consumer demand and ZEEKR's operational capabilities, positioning the company as a significant player in the global market. ZEEKR has also begun deliveries in Europe and plans to supply vehicles for the Waymo One Fleet in the U.S., highlighting its ambitions for international expansion. Overall, ZEEKR's trajectory reflects a robust commitment to innovation and market growth in the premium electric vehicle sector.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology began as a business unit within Geely Auto in October 2017. Lotus Technology conducts its business primarily through the following entities: (i) ZEEKR Automobile (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. (“ZEEKR Shanghai”), (ii) ZEEKR Automobile (Ningbo Hangzhou Bay New Zone) Co., Ltd. (“ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay”), (iii) Viridi E-Mobility Technology (Ningbo) Co., Ltd. (“Ningbo Viridi”) and (iv) Zeekr Technology Europe AB (“CEVT”). Under the leadership of the co-founders, Mr. Shufu Li, Mr. Conghui An, Mr. Donghui Li, and Mr. Shengyue Gui, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology was incorporated as an exempted company with limited liability in March 2021 under the law of the Cayman Islands to act as the holding company. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology is seeking to list separately from Geely Auto because of its different brand positioning and its operational, management, and financial independence. ZEEKR Intelligent Technology has an equity story built around its premium brand, product portfolio, and future plans that is better served by operating independently and seeking a separate listing, which the company believes will allow it to establish its own profile and attract different investors. In April 2021, ZEEKR Innovation, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Intelligent Technology, was incorporated under the laws of the British Virgin Islands. In the same period, ZEEKR Technology, currently a wholly-owned subsidiary of ZEEKR Innovation, was incorporated under the laws of Hong Kong. In April 2021, ZEEKR Intelligent Technology announced the launch of its first BEV model, ZEEKR 001, and started delivery from October 2021.

title: Our Corporate History 
summary: ZEEKR originated as a business unit within Geely Auto in October 2017 and has since evolved into a distinct entity with a focus on premium electric vehicles. Established as ZEEKR Intelligent Technology in March 2021 in the Cayman Islands, the company was co-founded by Mr. Shufu Li and others to pursue operational and financial independence from Geely Auto, allowing it to cultivate its own brand identity and attract diverse investors. The organization operates through several subsidiaries, including ZEEKR Automobile and ZEEKR Technology, and has made significant acquisitions to enhance its capabilities, such as acquiring ZEEKR Hangzhou Bay and CEVT from Geely Holding.

ZEEKR launched its first battery electric vehicle, the ZEEKR 001, in April 2021, followed by the ZEEKR 009 in November 2022 and the ZEEKR X compact SUV in April 2023. The company continues to expand its product lineup, introducing models like the ZEEKR 001 FR in October 2023 and an upscale sedan in January 2024. In April 2024, ZEEKR unveiled the ZEEKR 009 Grand, a luxury variant, and the ZEEKR MIX, a multi-purpose vehicle. This strategic focus on premium offerings and innovative designs positions ZEEKR as a competitive player in the electric vehicle market.

Lotus Technology notes that the company purchases vehicles for resale from Geely Holding at a price made up of the purchase cost of direct materials and a pre-agreed markup. Please describe the manufacturer’s warranty provided by Geely Holding for these vehicles and to what extent the manufacturer’s warranties have an impact on Lotus Technology's accounting for warranty expenses, if material. In response to the Staff’s comment, Lotus Technology has revised the disclosure on page 90 of the Revised Draft Registration Statement. Lotus Technology respectfully advises the Staff that under the cooperation framework agreement between Lotus Technology and Geely Holding, ZEEKR Factory provides an assurance-type product warranty for all product quality problems caused by its manufacturing, with a warranty period no less than what Lotus Technology offers to its customers. In the event where the product defect is caused by raw material suppliers, Lotus Technology is entitled to seek damages from ZEEKR Factory, which in turn could seek damages from the responsible suppliers. Lotus Technology further advises the Staff that when estimating warranties, the accrual is established based on estimates of the nature, frequency, and average costs of future claims, net of estimated warranty cost recoveries, to the extent that is estimable and probable.

metadata.title_summary:
title: How will you receive dividends and other distributions on the shares? 
summary: The depositary is responsible for distributing cash dividends and other distributions from shares or deposited securities to holders of American Depositary Shares (ADS), after deducting its fees and applicable taxes. Distributions are based on the number of shares represented by the ADSs held, and cash dividends may be converted into U.S. dollars if feasible; otherwise, foreign currency will be distributed to eligible holders. Any unconverted currency will be held without interest, and fractional cents will be rounded to the nearest whole cent. 

In cases where shares are issued as dividends, the depositary may distribute additional ADSs, sell fractional shares, or retain existing ADSs to represent new shares. The depositary also manages rights to purchase additional shares by either exercising the rights on behalf of ADS holders, distributing the rights, or selling them and sharing the proceeds after fees. If no action is taken, the rights will lapse.

For other distributed items related to deposited securities, the depositary may either sell them and distribute the proceeds or retain them, making the ADSs representative of the new property. However, it will only distribute non-ADS securities if it receives legal confirmation from the issuer. The depositary disclaims responsibility for distributions deemed unlawful or impractical and is not obligated to register ADSs or related securities under U.S. law, which may limit the distribution of certain securities to ADS holders. Thus, holders may not receive distributions if it is illegal or impractical for the depositary to provide them.

If no action is taken, the rights will lapse. The depositary must act only with legal assurances from the company and may face limitations under U.S. securities laws regarding the distribution of rights or securities.

Furthermore, the depositary is tasked with distributing any additional items related to the deposited securities, using methods it finds legal and practical. If direct distribution is not feasible, it may sell the items and distribute the proceeds or hold them as part of the ADS. However, it will only distribute non-ADS securities if legal confirmation is received. The depositary disclaims responsibility for distributions deemed unlawful or impractical and is not obligated to register any securities under the Securities Act, which may result in ADS holders not receiving certain distributions or associated values.

metadata.title_summary:
title: The disruption of supply or shortage of components and materials could have a material adverse impact on our business. 
summary: The company is at risk of supply interruptions and shortages of critical components and raw materials, such as steel, aluminum, semiconductor chips, and battery cells, which could adversely affect its business and financial performance. The supply of these materials is historically volatile, influenced by factors like increased competition in battery electric vehicle (BEV) production, ongoing U.S.-China trade tensions, and potential regulatory restrictions on suppliers. While recent U.S. export controls on advanced semiconductors may not have immediate effects, future regulations could limit sourcing options. Additionally, the growing demand for BEVs could exacerbate shortages and elevate material costs if production capacities for essential components do not keep pace. Should the company decide to raise product prices in response to these disruptions, it risks decreased sales, which could harm its brand reputation and overall business outlook.
